subtasks.description: Query SAM MongoDB for Slack contract records with renewal dates, terms, licensing model, and cost structure
Query consumption warehouse for Slack usage trends (active users, daily active users, message volume, file transfers) over the contract period and trailing months

Reconcile Slack contract renewals (SAM) with consumption trends (warehouse). Align renewal dates to usage phases, identify growth/decline patterns, classify renewal posture (growth, flat, contraction), and generate renewal trajectory assessment with cost-per-user and growth recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks are required: (1) mongo_query to retrieve Slack contract metadata from SAM, (2) sql_gen to aggregate consumption metrics from the warehouse, and (3) cross_recon to synthesize renewal trajectory by aligning contract events with usage patterns and generating posture classification. We avoid adding a separate policy_action since renewal posture is a data-driven reconciliation output, not a policy evaluation. mongo_query and sql_gen are assigned mid-tier: contract retrieval requires filtering by vendor and extracting renewal dates (moderate aggregation), and consumption requires time-series aggregation across user and activity dimensions. cross_recon is frontier-tier because it must perform multi-signal reconciliation (contract timeline + consumption trends), detect correlation patterns, classify posture, and synthesize recommendations with inference across two distinct data models.

output.sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) AS usage_month, cf.product, cf.event_type, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS active_users, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_seats, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, COUNT(cf.event_id) AS event_count, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.license_id) AS active_licenses FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%Slack%' OR cf.product ILIKE '%Slack%' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date), cf.product, cf.event_type ORDER BY usage_month ASC, cf.event_type
